Source: Emerging Markets Q2 2026 Outlook: Shifting Sands
Full Commentary: Aberdeen expects AI-related spending to accelerate in 2026, making AI a positive tailwind for APAC and EMs, while noting collapse risk is not its core view. Full Commentary: Aberdeen expects EM participation in the data-center buildout to broaden, highlighting India, Malaysia, and Gulf states as well placed once conflict abates. Source: Global Macro Outlook: Second Quarter 2026
Full Commentary: AB expects Middle East infrastructure damage to keep oil prices above prewar levels, making the oil price outlook positive despite uncertainty over duration. Evidence: We are not experts at geopolitics, or at assessing oil production. But it seems to us that the damage to oil-related infrastructure in the Middle East means that oil prices are likely to settle at least somewhat higher than before the war.. Locator: p.1.

Source: Global Macro Outlook: Second Quarter 2026
Full Commentary: AB is cautious on private markets because of opacity and possible excesses from rapid growth, while noting it does not expect a systemic crisis. Evidence: Private credit markets have also been in the spotlight, given their exposure to software companies. We share some of the concern in markets around the opacity of private markets, as well as potential excesses suggested by their explosive growth in recent years. Still, we think that any issues within private markets are unlikely to trigger a systemic crisis.. Locator: p.2.
